questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding safeguards under Article 22: I. A person detained preventively must be produced before a magistrate within 24 hours of detention. II. A person under preventive detention enjoys the right to consult and be defended by a legal practitioner of their choice. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding constitutional amendments in India: I. The abolition of a Legislative Council in a state requires a special majority of Parliament and ratification by half of the states. II. The distribution of legislative powers between the Union and the States can be altered only by a special majority of Parliament and ratification by half of the states. III. Salaries of Members of Parliament can be amended only by a special majority of Parliament since they involve financial implications. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the Basic Structure Doctrine: I. It was introduced by the Supreme Court in the Golak Nath case. II. The Court upheld that Parliament's amending power is absolute and unlimited in the Minerva Mills case. III. The Waman Rao case clarified that the doctrine of basic structure would apply to constitutional amendments enacted after April 24, 1973. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Parliament's power to legislate on matters in the State List: I. The Parliament can make laws on State List matters if Rajya Sabha passes a resolution by two-thirds majority and such laws remain in force for six months after the resolution lapses. II. When states request Parliament to legislate on a State List matter, the concerned state legislatures retain the power to amend or repeal that law. III. During President's Rule in a state, Parliament can legislate on State List matters for that state, but such laws automatically expire once President's Rule ends. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Zonal Councils in India: I. Zonal Councils are constitutional bodies created under Article 263 of the Constitution. II. Each Zonal Council is chaired by the Prime Minister of India and co-chaired by the Union Home Secretary. III. Zonal Councils are only deliberative and advisory bodies, aimed at promoting cooperation and coordination between states and the Centre. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: Article 358 automatically suspends the Fundamental Rights under Article 19 as soon as a National Emergency is proclaimed on the ground of war or external aggression. Statement II: Article 359 automatically suspends Fundamental Rights during a National Emergency, without requiring any Presidential Order. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following pairs: Type of Presidential Clemency - Meaning: I. Pardon - Removes both the conviction and the sentence. II. Commutation - Substitute one form of punishment for a lighter form. III. Remission - Reduce the period of sentence without changing its character. IV. Respite - Temporary stay of the execution of a sentence, especially death sentence. V. Reprieve - Award a lesser sentence due to special circumstances such as pregnancy or disability. How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Leaders in Parliament: I. The Leader of the House in Lok Sabha is always the Prime Minister, irrespective of whether he/she is a member of the Lok Sabha or not. II. The Leader of the Opposition is explicitly mentioned in the Constitution of India and enjoys the status of a Cabinet Minister. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: A Financial Bill (I) can be introduced only in the Lok Sabha and only on the recommendation of the President. Statement II: A Financial Bill (II) requires the recommendation of the President at the stage of its introduction in either House of Parliament. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the Judges of the Supreme Court: I. The President can directly appoint an ad hoc judge of the Supreme Court whenever there is a vacancy among its permanent judges. II. The chief justice of India can request a retired judge of the Supreme Court to act as a judge of the Supreme Court for a temporary period but this requires the prior consent of both the President and the judge concerned. III. An acting Chief Justice of India can be appointed only by the Chief Justice himself when he is unable to perform his duties. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the term of a Governor's office: I. The Constitution explicitly provides the grounds on which a Governor may be removed by the President. II. A Governor can continue to hold office even after the completion of his/her five-year term until a successor assumes charge. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the composition of Legislative Councils: Statement I: The maximum strength of a Legislative Council is fixed at one-third of the total strength of the Legislative Assembly, and its minimum strength is fixed at 40. Statement II: One-sixth of the members of a Legislative Council are directly elected by the people of the state through proportional representation. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: With reference to provisions ensuring the independence of High Courts in India, which of the following statements is not correct?

options.a: The pensions of High Court judges are charged on the Consolidated Fund of the State concerned.
options.b: The conduct of High Court judges in the discharge of duties cannot be discussed in Parliament or State Legislature except during an impeachment process.
options.c: Retired permanent judges of a High Court can practise in the other High Courts.
options.d: The jurisdiction and powers of a High Court specified in the Constitution cannot be curtailed by the Parliament.

questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Administrative Tribunals in India: I. Article 323A empowers both Parliament and State Legislatures to establish administrative tribunals. II. The Central Administrative Tribunal (CAT) covers service matters of all employees, including defence forces and the secretarial staff of Parliament. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission (NCDRC): I. The President and members are appointed by the Centre on the recommendation of a committee chaired by the Chief Justice of India or his nominee. II. It has pecuniary jurisdiction above 2 crore rupees, subject to change by the Centre. III. Appeals against its orders lie with the High Court. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Permanent Lok Adalats: I. It consists of a chairman who has held the office of a District Judge or higher. II. It can exercise jurisdiction even in matters relating to non-compoundable offences. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Who among the following was the founder of the Indian Association of Calcutta in 1876?
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements with regard to participation in the Civil Disobedience Movement: I. Women participated in large numbers, especially in picketing liquor shops and foreign cloth stores. II. Muslim participation remained high, similar to the level seen during the Non-Cooperation Movement. III. Merchants and traders actively supported the boycott calls, particularly in Tamil Nadu and Punjab. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements about Sir Syed Ahmed Khan: I. He wanted to harmonise Western scientific education with the teachings of Islam and believed that religion should adapt with time. II. He supported early Muslim political participation alongside Hindus to promote national unity. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. The partition of Bengal deliberately created two provinces with majoritarian religious groupings to foster communal division and weaken emerging nationalist unity. II. Nationalist leaders established institutions like the Bengal National College primarily to replace English-medium education with traditional Sanskrit-based learning. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the Government of India Act, 1935: I. The Council of States was to be partly directly elected from British Indian provinces and partly nominated by the princes. II. The Federal Assembly was to be partly indirectly elected from British Indian provinces and partly nominated by the princes. III. Dyarchy was introduced at the federal level. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: 'I must cling to my mother tongue as to my mother's breast, in spite of its shortcomings. It alone can give me the life-giving milk.' Who among the following made this statement?
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Who among the following was asked by the British East India Company to report on the progress of education in vernacular schools in Bengal and Bihar during the 1830s?
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Who among the following Gupta rulers assumed the title 'Maharajadhiraja' and married a Lichchhavi princess named Kumaradevi?
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Mauryan fiscal administration: I. The Sannidhata was the chief custodian of the state treasury and oversaw the management of revenues collected in both cash and kind. II. The State was responsible for establishing grain storage infrastructure due to the collection of a significant portion of revenue in kind. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Hiuen Tsang (Xuanzang), the Chinese Buddhist monk, visited India during the reign of
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the Farmans of Farrukhsiyar: I. In Bengal, the Company was exempted from additional customs duties on trade. II. East India Company's Bombay-minted coins were decreed to have currency throughout the Mughal empire. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the Rajah-Moonje Pact of 1932: I. The Pact proposed joint electorates with reserved seats for the Depressed Classes. II. The All India Depressed Classes Association, led by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, fully endorsed the Pact as representative of Dalit interests. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: Who among the following was part of the legal defence team for INA prisoners during their trials at the Red Fort?

options.a: Bhulabhai Desai and Tej Bahadur Sapru
options.c: C.R. Das and Kailash Nath Katju
options.d: Sardar Patel and Dr. Rajendra Prasad

questions.subject: History

questions.question_text: With reference to India's precise strikes and missile systems, consider the following pairs: System - Description: I. HAMMER - Air-to-ground precision-guided weapon system used for medium-range tactical strikes. II. SCALP - Air-launched cruise missile capable of long-range deep strikes. III. METEOR - Short-range, surface-to-air missile used primarily for anti-tank operations. IV. BRAHMOS - Supersonic cruise missile jointly developed by India and Russia. How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Fifth-Generation Fighter Aircraft: I. They are characterised by advanced stealth and beyond-visual-range (BVR) capabilities. II. The United States, Russia, India and China currently operate fifth-generation fighter aircraft. III. India's Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) is already operational and forms part of India's active air fleet. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: Which of the following methods can be used to destroy or neutralise hostile drones? I. Kinetic means II. Directed-Energy Weapons III. Electronic Warfare IV. Spoofing V. Cyber attacks VI. Interceptor drones and nets

options.d: I, II, III, IV, V and VI

questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. B-cells are responsible for the production of antibodies while T-cells mediate cell-mediated immune response. II. Helper T-cells assist B-cells in antibody production while Cytotoxic T-cells kill virus-infected and tumour cells. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: Viruses are considered to occupy the twilight zone between living and non-living organisms. Statement II: Viruses contain both types of nucleic acid, DNA and RNA. Statement III: Viruses depend on the host cell for replication as they lack the enzymes necessary for protein and nucleic acid synthesis. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Rare Earth Elements (REEs): I. India possesses deposits of rare earth elements but relies heavily on China for their refining and processing. II. Rare earth elements are essential for manufacturing products such as electric vehicles, wind turbines, and defence equipment. III. The mining and refining of rare earth elements cause minimal environmental damage due to their clean extraction processes. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. Artificial Intelligence is the overarching field that aims to make machines perform cognitive tasks such as reasoning, problem-solving, and learning like humans. II. Machine Learning is a subset of Artificial Intelligence that enables systems to learn from data and improve their performance without being explicitly programmed. III. Deep Learning is a subset of Machine Learning that uses multi-layered artificial neural networks to process complex patterns and large volumes of data. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: With reference to India's analog space missions and experiments, consider the following pairs: Mission - Objective: I. Gaganyaan Analog Experiments - To study human confinement, teamwork, and psychological aspects for astronaut training. II. Ladakh Human Analog Mission - To simulate human habitation in interplanetary conditions such as extreme cold and barren terrain. III. Tso Kar Valley Analog Mission - To replicate Mercury-like environmental conditions including high UV radiation and saline permafrost. IV. Bed-Rest Microgravity Simulation - To study physiological effects of prolonged microgravity on the human body under Earth-based conditions. How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: With reference to animals that thrive in Earth's harshest environments, consider the following pairs: Animal - Feature: I. Tardigrade - Survives extreme conditions by entering a dormant state called cryptobiosis. II. Arctic Fox - Changes fur colour seasonally and has a compact body to minimise heat loss. III. Tube Worm - Lives near hydrothermal vents and derives energy through chemosynthesis. IV. Yeti Crab - Cultivates bacteria on its claws as a food source near hydrothermal vents. How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO): I. The GSLV-F15 rocket used for ISRO's 100th launch is powered by an indigenously developed cryogenic engine. II. The LVM3 is currently India's heaviest operational launch vehicle and has been used for Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3 missions. III. The Next Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV) being developed by ISRO will have a reusable first stage. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the Genome India Project (GIP): Statement I: The Genome India Project maps India's genetic diversity to enable personalised medicine and better healthcare. Statement II: It identifies DNA variations to develop population-specific drugs and treatment plans. Statement III: It examines shared genetic traits among Indian groups. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding India's Small Modular Reactor (SMR) initiative: Statement I: Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) are compact nuclear reactors with capacities typically ranging between 30 MWe and 300 MWe, designed to provide low-carbon, reliable power for industrial and grid applications. Statement II: India's Bharat Small Modular Reactor (BSMR) and SMR-55 are both based on heavy-water reactor technology currently used in most of the country's operational units. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Sodium-ion battery technology: I. Sodium-ion batteries are cheaper and safer as sodium is more abundant and can be extracted from seawater. II. Sodium-ion batteries have a higher operating temperature range and are hence safer than lithium-ion batteries. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. Govind Ballabh Pant Sagar was created by damming the Rihand River. II. Kanwar Lake is formed by the meandering of the Gandak River. III. Periyar River directly feeds Vembanad Lake. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following pairs of tree species and their forest type in India: Tree species - Forest Type: I. Ebony - Evergreen. II. Babool - Thorn. III. Deodar - Deciduous. How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: 'This climate is characterized by hot, wet summers influenced by monsoon winds and mild, dry winters. The natural vegetation includes mixed forests of evergreen and deciduous species. Rice, cotton, and mulberry cultivation are prominent.' This is the most likely description of:
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: The study of earthquake waves provides vital information about the internal structure of the Earth. Statement II: P-waves can travel through both solids and liquids, while S-waves can travel only through solids. Statement III: The absence of S-waves beyond certain angular distances from the epicenter indicates the liquid nature of the Earth's outer core. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements about ocean currents: I. The Kuroshio Current in the North Pacific is a warm ocean current flowing northward along the coast of Japan. II. The Labrador Current is a cold ocean current that flows southwards along the coast of eastern Canada. III. The Humboldt Current is a warm ocean current that flows northwards along the western coast of South America. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following countries: I. Saudi Arabia II. Egypt III. Eritrea IV. Yemen V. Oman. How many of the above countries border the Red Sea?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: The term 'Maghreb' often heard in the news roughly corresponds to which of the following regions?

options.a: Region along the northwestern African shores
options.b: Region along the eastern Mediterranean shores
options.c: Region comprising the Horn of Africa and Red Sea littoral
options.d: The entire coastal areas of the Mediterranean Sea

questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements about Earth's movements: I. On the vernal equinox days, the Sun is directly overhead at the equator and all places on Earth experience nearly equal length of day and night. II. On the summer solstice day, the Sun is directly overhead at the Tropic of Cancer and the northern hemisphere experiences longest day and shortest night. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Which of the following countries are well known as the two largest producers of cobalt in the world?

options.b: Democratic Republic of Congo and Indonesia
options.c: Australia and Democratic Republic of Congo

questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following pairs: Landform - Type of Landform: I. Cirque - Glacial erosional. II. Delta - Fluvial depositional. III. Yardang - Aeolian depositional. IV. Spit - Marine depositional. How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: With reference to laterite soils in India, consider the following statements: I. They are formed in regions experiencing alternate wet and dry seasons under conditions of high temperature and heavy rainfall. II. Due to intense leaching, these soils are poor in nitrogen, phosphate, and lime but rich in iron and aluminium oxides. III. With proper irrigation and manuring, they support plantation crops such as tea, coffee, cashew, and coconut. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: Alfred Wegener's Continental Drift Theory proposed that all present-day continents were once joined together as a single landmass called Pangaea. Statement II: Wegener explained the drift of continents primarily through convection currents in the mantle, which was later universally accepted. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: With reference to the Himalayan ranges, which one of the following is the correct sequence from South to North?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: With reference to the Asthenosphere, consider the following statements: I. It extends from the lower part of the crust to the core-mantle boundary and acts as a liquid layer for plate movement. II. It is characterized as a low-velocity zone for seismic waves due to partial melting of rocks. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following pairs of crops and their major producing states in India: Crops - Major Producing State: I. Jowar - Maharashtra. II. Groundnut - Gujarat. III. Soyabean - Madhya Pradesh. IV. Tea - Kerala. How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: With reference to the Genetic Engineering Appraisal Committee (GEAC), consider the following statements: I. The GEAC functions under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, Government of India. II. GEAC approval is mandatory for the environmental release, field trials, and commercial cultivation of genetically modified organisms (GMOs) in India. III. The GEAC is the competent authority for approving the import, export, manufacture, and sale of genetically engineered organisms and their products in India. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: With reference to Dugong, consider the following statements: I. It is listed as 'Critically Endangered' in the IUCN Red List. II. It is the only herbivorous marine mammal found in Indian waters. III. Its global range is restricted only to the Indian Ocean region. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following pairs regarding Gravitational Wave Observatories and their primary host/location: Observatory - Location: I. Virgo - Tuscany, Italy. II. KAGRA - Hida city, Japan. III. LIGO-India - Karnataka, India. How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: The term 'Gwada Negative' recently seen in news refers to:
questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: Zangezur Corridor is a proposed multimodal transport route, recently in the news, which will connect

options.a: Armenia with Iran through the Nagorno-Karabakh region
options.b: Azerbaijan to Nakhchivan region through Armenia

questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the Dharti Abba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan (DAJGUA): I. It is implemented only in the states classified as aspirational districts by NITI Aayog. II. The campaign includes real-time digital monitoring and community audits to ensure transparency at the village level. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: With reference to the International Organization for Marine Aids to Navigation (IALA), consider the following statements: I. The transition of IALA to an Intergovernmental Organization (IGO) in 2024 primarily focused on allowing IALA to admit new Industrial Members. II. IALA was established in 1957 as a Non-Governmental Organization (NGO) before attaining its current status. III. India is set to host the 21st IALA Conference in 2027 in Gujarat. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to the National Sports Governance Act, 2025, consider the following statements: I. The regulation of sports in India falls under the concurrent jurisdiction of both the Union and the States. II. A recognised sports organisation receiving government grants shall be considered as a public authority under Right to Information Act, 2005 with respect to utilisation of such grants. III. The National Sports Tribunal established under the Act will not have jurisdiction over disputes relating to games organized by international bodies. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. The FIDE World Cup 2025 is scheduled to be held in Goa, India. II. Divya Deshmukh became the first Indian woman to win the FIDE Women's World Cup title. III. Grandmaster Praggnanandhaa Rameshbabu became the first Indian player after Viswanathan Anand to reach the final of the FIDE World Cup. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Which of the following organizations publish the World Economic Situation and Prospects report?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. In most terrestrial ecosystems, the pyramid of biomass is upright. II. In aquatic ecosystems, the pyramid of biomass is often inverted. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: The phosphorus cycle differs from the carbon and nitrogen cycles because it does not have a significant atmospheric component. Statement II: Phosphorus exists mainly in the earth's crust as phosphate rocks, and it enters the biogeochemical cycle through weathering and erosion. Statement III: Phosphorus rarely forms gaseous compounds under natural conditions. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. Fringing reefs are directly attached to the shore and are the most common type of coral reef found in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. II. Barrier reefs run parallel to coastlines, separated from the shore by a lagoon, and are majorly found in the Gulf of Mannar. III. Atolls are circular or semicircular reefs formed by coral growth on subsiding sea floor platforms, such as those seen in Lakshadweep. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following pollutants: I. Carbon monoxide (CO) II. Suspended Particulate Matter (SPM) III. Sulphur dioxide (SO2) IV. Ground-level ozone (O3). Which of the above are primarily released from vehicular emissions?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. Phytoplankton are microscopic plant organisms that occur only in marine waters. II. Like land plants, most phytoplankton possess chlorophyll and perform photosynthesis, releasing oxygen. III. The total oxygen production by phytoplankton is equal to or higher than that produced by land vegetation. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. A National Park may be declared by the State Government to protect areas of ecological and faunal importance. II. The boundaries of a National Park may be altered by the State Government after consultation with the State Board for Wildlife. III. A Conservation Reserve may be notified by the State Government after consultation with local communities, especially for areas adjoining National Parks or Sanctuaries. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the environmental impact of DDT (Dichloro-Diphenyl-Trichloroethane): Statement I: DDT is a persistent organic pollutant that accumulates through biomagnification. Statement II: DDT is chemically stable and fat-soluble. Statement III: Its concentration is high in top-level predators. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands: Statement I: The Ramsar Convention, signed in 1971, is the only global treaty that focuses exclusively on the conservation and wise use of wetlands. Statement II: The Convention operates under the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) as part of the multilateral biodiversity agreements system. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Green Hydrogen: Statement I: Green Hydrogen is regarded as a key enabler for global decarbonisation. Statement II: It is mainly produced through the electrolysis of water using electricity derived from renewable sources such as solar or wind energy. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. Mangroves are evergreen plants that can tolerate moderate salinity but require continuous tidal influence for their survival. II. In mangroves, vivipary is an adaptive mechanism that helps seedlings establish in soft, saline, and unstable mudflats. III. All mangrove species develop prop roots that rise vertically above the ground to facilitate gaseous exchange in waterlogged conditions. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Marginal Standing Facility (MSF): I. Under MSF, scheduled commercial banks can borrow overnight funds from RBI by dipping into their SLR portfolio. II. It provides a safety valve against unanticipated liquidity shocks to the banking system. III. The MSF rate is lower than the Repo rate to encourage banks to borrow easily in times of liquidity crunch. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Economics

questions.question_text: With reference to Priority Sector Lending in India, consider the following: I. Agriculture II. Renewable Energy III. Housing IV. Export Credit. How many of the above are included under Priority Sector categories?

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: Current Account transactions do not alter a country's assets or liabilities. Statement II: Current Account includes trade in goods and services, unilateral transfers, and investment income. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.subject: Economics

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. Treasury bills are short-term zero-coupon securities issued by the Government of India and are traded in the money market. II. Dated Government securities generally have a tenor of 5 years to 40 years. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Economics

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: I. Purchasing Power Parity exchange rate refers to the rate at which one currency can be exchanged for another without adjusting for price differences. II. Nominal exchange rate adjusts currencies so that the same basket of goods costs the same in two countries. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Economics

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: GDP at market prices tends to exceed GDP at factor cost when indirect taxes are greater than subsidies. Statement II: GDP at market prices accounts for government-imposed taxes and subsidies, which alter the prices consumers pay without changing the underlying cost of production. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.subject: Economics

questions.question_text: Consider the following financial instruments: I. Equity shares II. Fully and compulsorily convertible debentures III. Fully and compulsorily convertible preference shares. How many of the above are treated as eligible capital instruments for Foreign Direct Investment?

questions.question_text: Consider the following: I. Total Expenditure II. Revenue Receipts III. Non-debt Capital Receipts IV. Market Borrowings. How many of the above are directly used to compute Fiscal Deficit?

questions.question_text: The Laffer Curve

options.a: Shows that government revenue always rises with increasing tax rates
options.b: Illustrates that government revenue rises with tax rates up to a point and then falls if rates rise further
options.c: Suggests that reducing tax rates always increases economic output without affecting revenue
options.d: Depicts a direct proportional relationship between tax rates and government expenditure

questions.subject: Economics

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Black plastic: I. It is an indicator species of clean and healthy freshwater ecosystems. III. It is not found in the Indian subcontinent. How many of the above given statements are correct?

Medical practitioners and diagnostic laboratories are legally required to report cases of notifiable diseases to government authorities. II. The list of notifiable diseases is determined by the Central Government for all states and union territories.

They must contest an election within five years of registration to remain on the list of registered parties. How many of the statements given above are correct?

The office is modelled on the lines of the American Vice-President.

If the election of the President of India is declared void by the Supreme Court of India, all acts done by him/her in the performance of duties of his/her office of President before the date of decision become invalid. 2. Election for the post of the President of India can be postponed on the ground that some Legislative Assemblies have been dissolved and elections are yet to take place. 3. When a Bill is presented to the President of India, the Constitution prescribes time limits within which he/she has to declare his/her assent. How many of the above statements are correct?

questions.question_text: If a particular area is brought under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India, which one of the following statements best reflects the consequence of it?

options.a: This would prevent the transfer of land of tribal people to non-tribal people.
options.b: This would create a local self-governing body in that area.
options.c: This would convert that area into a Union Territory.
options.d: The State having such areas would be declared a Special Category State.

questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: 1. The Constitution of India classifies the ministers into four ranks viz. Cabinet Minister, Minister of State with Independent Charge, Minister of State and Deputy Minister. 2. The total number of ministers in the Union Government, including the Prime Minister, shall not exceed 15 percent of the total number of members in the Lok Sabha. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: With reference to India, consider the following statements: 1. Judicial custody means an accused is in the custody of the concerned magistrate and such accused is locked up in a police station, not in jail. 2. During judicial custody, the police officer in charge of the case is not allowed to interrogate the suspect without the approval of the court. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: 1. It is the Governor of the State who recognizes and declares any community of that State as a Scheduled Tribe. 2. A community declared as a Scheduled Tribe in a State need not be so in another State. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements are correct about the Constitution of India? 1. Powers of the Municipalities are given in Part IX A of the Constitution. 2. Emergency provisions are given in Part XVIII of the Constitution. 3. Provisions related to the amendment of the Constitution are given in Part XX of the Constitution.

questions.question_text: The Ninth Schedule was introduced in the Constitution of India during the prime ministership of
questions.subject: Polity

options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I

options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I

options.d: Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I

options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I

options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I

options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
options.c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect

options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I

options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

Statement III: Article 6 of the Paris Agreement on climate change intends to promote inter-country non-market strategies to reach their climate targets. options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct but only one of them explains Statement I

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: 1. Jhelum River passes through Wular Lake. 2. Krishna River directly feeds Kolleru Lake. 3. Meandering of Gandak River formed Kanwar Lake. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following pairs: Reservoirs - States: 1. Ghataprabha - Telangana. 2. Gandhi Sagar - Madhya Pradesh. 3. Indira Sagar - Andhra Pradesh. 4. Maithon - Chhattisgarh. How many pairs given above are not correctly matched?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: With reference to 'Coriolis force', which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. It increases with increase in wind velocity. 2. It is maximum at the poles and is absent at the equator.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following pairs: Objects in space - Description: 1. Cepheids - Giant clouds of dust and gas in space. 2. Nebulae - Stars which brighten and dim periodically. 3. Pulsars - Neutron stars that are formed when massive stars run out of fuel and collapse. How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Science and Technology

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: Statement I: In January, in the Northern Hemisphere, the isotherms bend equatorward while crossing the landmasses, and poleward while crossing the oceans. Statement II: In January, the air over the oceans is warmer than that over the landmasses in the Northern Hemisphere. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: With reference to Ocean Mean Temperature (OMT), which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. OMT is measured up to a depth of 26 degree C isotherm which is 129 meters in the south-western Indian Ocean during January-March. 2. OMT collected during January-March can be used in assessing whether the amount of rainfall in monsoon will be less or more than a certain long-term mean.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following countries: 1. Bulgaria 2. Czech Republic 3. Hungary 4. Latvia 5. Lithuania 6. Romania. How many of the above-mentioned countries share a land border with Ukraine?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following information: Waterfall - Region - River: 1. Dhuandhar - Malwa - Narmada. 2. Hundru - Chota Nagpur - Subarnarekha. 3. Gersoppa - Western Ghats - Netravati. In how many of the above rows is the given information correctly matched?
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements: 1. India's first-ever Polar Research Vessel is being co-designed and built indigenously by Garden Reach Shipbuilders and Engineers Limited (GRSE). 2. India currently operates Maitri polar research station in the Arctic region. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to the International Institute of Administrative Sciences (IIAS), consider the following statements: 1. India has won the Presidency of the International Institute of Administrative Sciences (IIAS) for the term 2025-2028. 2. It is a formally affiliated body of the United Nations. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Chicago Convention, recently in the news, is related to:

options.a: International maritime safety and prevention of oil pollution
options.b: International civil aviation standards and investigation of aircraft accidents
options.c: Regulation of international space travel and satellite navigation
options.d: International liability regime for nuclear accidents

questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to the At Sea Observer Mission launched by the QUAD nations, consider the following statements: 1. Officers from QUAD nations participated in a cross-embarkation mission on a United States Coast Guard ship. 2. It was undertaken under the Wilmington Declaration. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to the Employment Linked Incentive (ELI) Scheme, consider the following statements: 1. First-time employees registered with The Employees Provident Fund Organisation are eligible to receive one month's wage (up to 15000 rupees) through Direct Benefit Transfer. 2. The scheme provides incentives to employers in all sectors, with a special focus on the services sector. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to the RECLAIM Framework, consider the following statements: 1. It is a community engagement and development framework designed for mine closure and post-closure phases. 2. It has been developed by the Central Institute of Mining and Fuel Research (CIMFR) Dhanbad. 3. The framework focuses only on environmental restoration and does not address community participation or livelihoods. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: With reference to the Indo-Burma Ramsar Regional Initiative (IBRRI), consider the following statements: 1. It was jointly developed by the Ramsar National Focal Points of five Indo-Burma countries along with IUCN's Asia Regional Office. 2. Its Secretariat is hosted by the Ramsar Convention Secretariat in Geneva. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: US Entity List, recently in news, is discussed in the context of:
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Mission Mausam: 1. It aims to develop improved earth system models, and data-driven methods (use of AI/ML). 2. It is being implemented by institutions under the Ministry of Earth Sciences, including the India Meteorological Department. 3. The Mission focuses mainly on long-term climate negotiations at the international level. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to INS Nilgiri, consider the following statements: 1. It belongs to the Shivalik class of frigates currently in service with the Indian Navy. 2. It is the first ship of the Nilgiri-class stealth frigates being built under Project 17A. 3. These frigates are designed mainly for coastal operations close to the Indian shoreline. 4. The ships under Project 17A are equipped with a Medium Range Surface-to-Air Missile (MRSAM) system. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to the Unified Pension Scheme (UPS), consider the following statements: 1. It has been notified as an option to the National Pension System (NPS) for Central Government employees. 2. Under the scheme, the government's contribution is increased to 18.5% of the combined basic pay and dearness allowance, while the employee's contribution remains unchanged. 3. The scheme provides a full assured pension payout only after completion of 10 years of qualifying service. 4. The Unified Pension Scheme consists of both an individual corpus and a pool corpus, with contributions from the Central Government. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question_text: With reference to the National Critical Minerals Mission, consider the following statements: 1. It aims to intensify the exploration of critical minerals within India as well as in offshore areas. 2. Under the mission, funds have been allocated to the National Mineral Exploration Trust (NMET) to support exploration activities outside India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Consider the following: 1. Public service AI 2. Future of work 3. Innovation and culture 4. Trust in AI. How many of the above were major themes of the Paris AI Summit 2025?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Transforming Relationship Utilizing Strategic Technology (TRUST), a bilateral initiative for cooperation in the recovery and processing of critical minerals, is a pact signed between which of the following countries?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to the Prime Minister Dhan-Dhaanya Krishi Yojana (PMDKY), consider the following statements: 1. The scheme targets districts identified on the basis of low agricultural productivity, moderate crop intensity and below-average credit parameters. 2. It seeks to promote crop diversification and sustainable agricultural practices. 3. It has been designed on the lines of Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY). Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the International Big Cat Alliance (IBCA): 1. The five signatory countries that have deposited the instruments of ratification of the Framework Agreement are India, Nicaragua, Liberia, Eswatini and Somalia. 2. The Alliance covers the conservation of seven big cats: lion, tiger, leopard, cheetah, snow leopard, jaguar and puma. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following: 1. Adoption of the BIMSTEC Kuala Lumpur Vision 2030. 2. Signing of the Agreement on Maritime Transport Cooperation. 3. Signing of a Memorandum of Understanding with the Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) and the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC). How many of the above were outcomes of the 6th BIMSTEC Summit held in 2025?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to the Greenhouse Gas Emission Intensity (GEI) Target Rules, 2025, consider the following statements: 1. The Rules set legally binding emission intensity targets for four high-emission sectors: aluminium, cement, chlor-alkali and steel. 2. The Rules aim to operationalise India's domestic carbon market under the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS), 2023. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: With reference to the India-UK maritime exercise Konkan-25, consider the following statements: 1. It is the first-ever joint maritime exercise involving the Carrier Strike Groups of India and the United Kingdom. 2. The Indian Carrier Strike Group participated in the exercise was led by the indigenous aircraft carrier INS Vikrant. 3. The exercise is conducted annually and is named after the coastal region of India. 4. The exercise was conducted in both harbour and sea phases. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to the IUCN World Conservation Congress, consider the following statements: 1. The IUCN World Conservation Congress 2025 was held in Abu Dhabi. 2. It is organised every three years to allow IUCN member organisations to decide key issues and actions related to nature conservation. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: With reference to the World Health Summit (WHS) 2025, consider the following statements: 1. The theme of the World Health Summit 2025 was 'Taking Responsibility for Health in a Fragmenting World'. 2. Professor Balvir S. Tomar, founder and Chancellor of NIMS University, Jaipur, served as the International President of WHS 2025. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to the Synchronous All India Elephant Estimation (SAIEE) 2021-25, consider the following statements: 1. It reported over 22,000 elephants in India, with Karnataka having the largest population. 2. Compared to 2017, the elephant population has declined, with the highest drop recorded in Jharkhand and Odisha. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the Global Forest Resources Assessment (GFRA) 2025: 1. India accounts for 2% of the world's forest areas. 2. India has climbed to the seventh spot globally in total forest area. 3. India has retained its third rank in annual forest area gain. How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.subject: Environment

questions.question_text: With reference to the Rashtriya Vigyan Puraskar, consider the following statements: 1. It is conferred in four categories - Vigyan Ratna, Vigyan Shri, Vigyan Yuva and Vigyan Team. 2. The Vigyan Shri honours lifetime contributions to science and technology, while the Vigyan Ratna recognises exceptional work in specific fields. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding the Export Promotion Mission (EPM): 1. It is a ten-year Mission to support sectors impacted by recent global tariff escalations. 2. The Mission operates through two integrated sub-schemes: Niryat Protsahan and Niryat Disha. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: With reference to the Belem Health Action Plan, consider the following statements: 1. It was unveiled on the dedicated Health Day of the UNFCCC COP30 under Brazil's Presidency. 2. The Plan focuses on strengthening global health systems in the context of climate change. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Consider the following initiatives: 1. G20 Global Healthcare Response Team 2. Global Traditional Knowledge Repository 3. Open Satellite Data Partnership 4. Critical Minerals Circularity Initiative. How many of the above were proposed by India at the recent G20 Summit in South Africa?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Munduruku Indigenous group, recently in the news, primarily belong to which of the following countries?
questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: The Silver Notice, recently launched by INTERPOL, is specifically designed to assist member countries in addressing:

options.a: terrorism financing and money laundering networks
options.c: recovery of proceeds of crime linked to organised criminal groups
options.d: illegal migration and human trafficking operations

questions.subject: Current Affairs

questions.question_text: Consider the following statements regarding Fostering Effective Energy Transition report 2025: 1. Under the Energy Transition Index (ETI) framework, system performance is assessed across the dimensions of security, equity, and sustainability. 2. Emerging Europe and emerging Asia followed identical pathways in improving their transition readiness under the ETI assessment. 3. Global energy security stagnated due to factors such as limited diversification of energy sources, high import dependence, and inflexible power systems. How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.subject: Current Affairs
